outbound_strategy.reply_style: Genuine and conversational. Compliment specific image qualities (lighting, composition, style). Use short, natural sentences. Avoid generic praise — reference something visible in their post.

ab_test.variant_a: Ultra-short captions (1-5 words) mimicking top performers like @yuumispm and @imrubyreid who post image-first with minimal text
ab_test.variant_b: Engagement-question captions (6-15 words) using question hooks like 'would you?' or 'rate me 1-10' based on trending post patterns

key_insights: Top EN performers (@imrubyreid 48K likes, @yuumispm 32K likes) succeed with image-first posts and ultra-short captions — visual quality drives engagement, not text length.

Engagement-bait questions ('would you?', 'rate me 1-10', 'how many pounds should I lose?') consistently appear in trending posts (6 of 12 viral posts use this pattern) — incorporate question hooks into 25% of posts.
92.7% of competitors use zero hashtags; the few hashtag-heavy accounts (#SFW, #digitalart) are mid-tier performers — minimize hashtag usage to 0-2 per post to match top performer behavior.

Reply contamination is 36.9% across competitors — many accounts inflate engagement rates through reply conversations. Focus on organic feed post engagement rather than reply chains for authentic growth.
EN market avg posting frequency is 4.3/day but top performers (@sessypuuh, @yuumispm) post only 0.7/day with much higher per-post quality — quality over quantity is the proven EN strategy.
Newly discovered @AndrewDark350 (208K followers) and @waifu4u2 (76K) represent potential collaboration or engagement targets in the #AIgirl space.
risks: Engagement-bait captions may attract low-quality followers who don't convert to long-term engagement — monitor follower retention after A/B test.
Low posting frequency (4 posts/day) means each post must be high quality; a single poor-performing post significantly impacts daily metrics.
Competition is fierce in EN market (avg 140K followers) — differentiation through unique visual style is essential to stand out.

outbound_strategy.reply_style: Japanese-natural, friendly tone. Use casual polite form (ですます). Compliment specific aesthetic elements. Keep replies warm and genuine — avoid overly formal or robotic language. One emoji maximum.

ab_test.variant_a: 3 posts per day with high visual quality focus, matching EN-style quality-over-quantity approach (days 1-2: skip slot 4)
ab_test.variant_b: 4 posts per day at scheduled times with standard quality (days 3-5: full schedule)

key_insights: JP market has lower avg engagement (0.23% follower-based) but impression-based rates are actually higher (6.32% avg) than EN (5.06%) — JP posts reach fewer followers but those who see them engage more. Focus on maximizing reach through algorithm-friendly posting.

Top JP performer @ganbarukaisyain (338K followers) uses zero hashtags and posts infrequently (0.3/day) — extreme quality-over-quantity approach works even in the high-frequency JP market where avg is 6.6 posts/day.
@tororo_223 achieved 1,529 likes with only 11K followers (16.8% engagement rate), the highest reply-adjusted rate in JP market — study their content style for template reference.

JP market is heavily reply-contaminated: @hakoirikumatan, @2nd_karen_AI, @seisei_ai_1st have 100% reply rates with zero organic feed posts — avoid this community-chat trap that produces zero organic growth.
JP market has lower competition density (67K avg followers vs 140K EN) and fewer competitors (17 vs 26) — there is more room for a quality-focused newcomer to establish presence.
JP hashtag ecosystem (#AIイラスト, #AI美女, #SFW) is slightly more active than EN, but still used by only mid-tier accounts. Keep hashtag usage minimal (0-2 per post) to align with top performer patterns.
risks: JP audience expects consistent posting rhythm — the A/B test of reduced frequency (variant A) may cause temporary follower loss if audience expects daily content.
Reply contamination patterns are culturally ingrained in JP AI art community — must actively resist the temptation to grow through reply chains rather than organic feed posts.
JP market #SFW tag association means content must stay strictly clean — any borderline content risks community backlash and potential account issues.
